name: From Tiñosillos to El Bohodón — circular by the Camino de El Chorrillo
name_original: De Tiñosillos a El Bohodón — circular por el Camino de El Chorrillo
region_intro: Hiking around Tiñosillos offers routes through the flat, agricultural landscape of the Ávila province, often following river courses or connecting small rural communities. The terrain is generally level with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for accessible walks. Many trails are circular, providing convenient starting and ending points within the region. Best hiking trails around Tiñosillos The most popular hiking route is Arévalo River Walk, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete, following the river through open countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo — circular by Pedro Rodríguez, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.5 km) path. This circular route explores the rural surroundings and takes approximately 3 hours 11 minutes. Local hikers also love the From Tiñosillos to El Bohodón — circular by the Camino de El Chorrillo, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through agricultural fields and connecting villages, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Hiking around Tiñosillos is defined by river paths, open fields, and circular walks connecting rural towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy hikes near Tiñosillos. The routes in Tiñosillos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 18 hikers have used komoot to explore Tiñosillos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Tiñosillos?
A: The hiking trails around Tiñosillos are generally characterized by a flat, agricultural landscape. You'll often find routes following river courses or connecting small rural communities. The terrain is mostly level with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for accessible walks.

Q: Are there many easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Tiñosillos?
A: Yes, Tiñosillos offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 5 minutes to complete, following the river through open countryside.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Tiñosillos area?
A: Many of the routes around Tiñosillos are circular, providing convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248233708/ermita-del-cristo-de-los-pinares-from-nava-de-arevalo-circular-by-pedro-rodriguez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo — circular by Pedro Rodríguez&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.8-mile (12.5 km) path exploring rural surroundings.

Q: What are some family-friendly hiking options near Tiñosillos?
A: Given the generally flat and accessible terrain, many trails are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt; is a great easy option. Another good choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146324807/from-tinosillos-to-el-bohodon-circular-by-the-camino-de-el-chorrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Tiñosillos to El Bohodón — circular by the Camino de El Chorrillo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.2 miles (6.8 km) and leads through agricultural fields, connecting villages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Tiñosillos?
A: Most trails in the agricultural and rural areas around Tiñosillos are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through farmlands or near livestock. Always carry water for your pet.

Q: What kind of attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking near Tiñosillos?
A: While hiking, you can explore several points of interest. Near some routes, you might find historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5856915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Villa and Church of San Martín in Arévalo&lt;/a&gt;. You can also enjoy natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5768284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arevalillo Riverbank&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any hidden gem hiking trails in the Tiñosillos area?
A: While not widely known, the routes connecting small villages offer a glimpse into local life and serene landscapes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31500364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bohodón loop from Villanueva de Gómez&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.1-mile (9.8 km) hike that takes you through quiet rural settings.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tiñosillos?
A: The best time to hike in Tiñosillos is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is either blooming or showcasing autumnal colors. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, though the flat terrain makes winter hiking feasible on clear days.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Tiñosillos?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails available in the Tiñosillos area, catering to various difficulty levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 8 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Tiñosillos?
A: The trails in Tiñosillos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the quiet, accessible nature of the routes and the opportunity to explore the peaceful rural landscape.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking trailheads near Tiñosillos?
A: Public transport options directly to specific trailheads might be limited in this rural area. It's generally advisable to plan for personal vehicle use or check local bus schedules for connections to the main villages like Tiñosillos or Arévalo, from where many routes begin.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails around Tiñosillos?
A: Parking is typically available in the villages where the trails start, such as Tiñosillos, Arévalo, or Villanueva de Gómez. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots, always being mindful of local regulations and private property.
tips: Population that is located at the gates of what is called Campo de Pajares on its eastern side. Pine forests that occupy a large area.

El Bohodón is a municipality in Spain belonging to the province of Ávila, in the autonomous community of Castilla y León. It is located in the La Moraña region, in the north of the province. It is located 35 kilometers from the capital and 17 kilometers from Arévalo.
